pattern: Rooting the tree and folding each subtree's value upward lets a single bottom-up pass decide, node by node, whether the piece hanging below can be sliced off as its own divisible component or must keep merging into its parent.
hints: Think of the tree as rooted at any single node. If you know the total value hanging below a node (its own value plus whatever wasn't already sliced away from its children), you know immediately whether that whole hanging piece could become its own component.

Process nodes from the leaves upward. For a node, add in the leftover value from each child that could NOT be sliced off on its own; a child that already formed a clean divisible piece contributes nothing further upward, since it was already cut away.

Once a node's accumulated value (its own value plus the leftovers of its children) is divisible by k, count that as one finished component and let nothing pass further up from it; the root itself always closes out one final component this way, since the whole tree's total is guaranteed divisible by k.

edge_inputs.description: a lone node with no edges at all
a lone node whose value already matches k exactly
a star of all zero values splits into every node on its own
a short chain where every node's own value already matches k
a short chain that is only ever divisible as the whole tree
a small branching tree where every value is even so every cut is legal
two nodes each already at the maximum allowed value
k equal to one, so any split at all is legal

stress.description: large random tree at the maximum allowed node count, sum forced divisible by k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 30000))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([parent, i])
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    values = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    remainder = sum(values) % k
    if remainder != 0:
        values[-1] += (k - remainder)
    return [n, edges, values, k]

stress.description: a long path graph (worst case for recursion depth), sum forced divisible by k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 30000))
    edges = [[i - 1, i] for i in range(1, n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 500)
    values = [rng.randint(0, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    remainder = sum(values) % k
    if remainder != 0:
        values[-1] += (k - remainder)
    return [n, edges, values, k]
